title: Coming from Colorado to farm; women getting sick from picking peas
description: They came from Colorado in 1928 so husband could farm. Some women got sick "picking peas", so preferred working for Dr. Robinson.

title: Workers liking Dr. Robinson but not the townspeople; Dr. Robinson didn't want local people involved with lessons
description: His workers liked Dr. Robinson very much, but townspeople were against him. He was a very charitable giver for Moscow, but didn't want it known. His lessons were good, but workers weren't supposed to read them. He didn't want any local people involved.

title: Pressures to produce more; set up of Psychiana building and how they got their mailing lists
description: Pressures to produce more. Set-up of Psychiana buildings and mailing operation. Psychiana purchased mailing lists from all kinds of companies. Many students from big cities. The lessons.

title: Working for sixty dollars a month in 1940; work policies on breaks and missing work; how many people and who worked there
description: She started at about $60 a month (c. 1940). They weren't docked for missing work for sickness. One fifteen minute break a day. As many as fifty people worked there. Many of the girls were putting their husbands through school.

title: Dr. Robinson's help for others; Moscow's feelings Dr. Robinson; other spiritual leaders didn't associate with him; people being jealous of Dr. Robinson
description: Robinson's help for others. Moscow's feeling of superiority as a town She told people not to speak ill of Robinson. His youth center. Moscow's coldness. He was spiritual. Ministers in Moscow didn't associate with him. People were jealous of the fancy cars he drove. His defenders kept quiet about it.

title: Florence Robinson loved working in the office in the summer; throwing a party for all the help; Florence Robinson's marriage
description: Florence Robinson's summer working in the office which she loved. She had the Robinsons' throw a party for all the help. Her marriage.

title: Alfred Robinson asking the workers to help explain the business; closing of Psychiana; Robinson criticized by local businessmen; minsters' hostility towards him; his appearance
description: Alfred Robinson asked the workers to explain what they did to help him understand the business. Closing of Psychiana. Robinson's sincerity. Businessmen who criticized him weren't on the up-and-up themselves. The Robinson's commonness. He spoke his mind; the ministers' hostility towards him. Support of his workers for him. His appearance.

title: Friendly neighbors in Troy versus Moscow; Moscow's failure to provide student housing; Churches in Moscow
description: Neighborliness of Troy community compared to Moscow An exchange a newcomer about growth in the area. Moscow's failure to provide housing for students. Churches in Moscow are overly separate.
